message:
fix(guard+core): wrap remaining pre-push git calls in SIGSEGV retry + reap child on stdin-write error

guard/lib.rs — get_push_paths missed the retry wrapper on three paths
------------------------------------------------------------------------
The pre-push hook handler (`get_push_paths`) ran git three ways, none of
which went through `guard_run_git_with_retry`:

  1. `git rev-list --topo-order <range>` — enumerate commits in push range
  2. `git diff-tree ... <sha>` — per-commit path enumeration (preferred)
  3. `git diff --name-status -M -z <range>` — fallback net diff

All three are exactly the kind of short-lived git invocation that can
trip the 2.51.0 `.git/index` mmap race and SIGSEGV, and all three were
being called with bare `.output()?`. That meant any segfault during a
pre-push hook ran would surface as a hook-level failure even though
the fix for this exact shape — a brief retry — is already implemented
right above. Rerouted all three through `guard_run_git_with_retry` so
the policy is consistent with `get_staged_paths` (pre-commit).

core/git_cmd.rs — reap child on stdin.write_all error
------------------------------------------------------
In `run_child`, if the child was spawned with a piped stdin and
`stdin.write_all(bytes)` failed (EPIPE, broken pipe, disk-full on a
large input, etc.), we called `child.kill()` but returned without
reaping. That leaks a zombie for the rest of the server's lifetime,
same shape as the `try_wait` errors fixed in bfc2d913.

The fix also drops the stdin handle BEFORE reaping. Without the drop,
`child.wait()` could block waiting for a child that is itself blocked
reading stdin. Dropping the handle closes the write-end, the child
sees EOF, exits, and wait() returns promptly.

No behavior change on the happy path; both fixes only touch error paths.
